purpose: Seeded sector and subsector context catalog used to amplify, suppress, or invalidate strategy family selection, lead ranking, and research routing.

granularityByLayer.research: Sector packages retain subsector lineage, linked catalysts, and cross-sector sympathy maps.
granularityByLayer.strategic: Downstream layers receive sector bias strength, veto signals, and high-level subsector leadership only.
granularityByLayer.tactical: Strategy bias overlays, invalidate conditions, and sector-specific range hints expand into branch planning.
granularityByLayer.training_feedback: Replay loops may refresh sector reliability and bias strength, but only inside seeded envelope limits.

runtimeControlSurface.policyRule: Sector packages bias ranking and threshold selection upstream, but they never replace deterministic legality or guardrail checks at dispatch time.

sectors.summary: Leadership-heavy sector with rapid theme rotation across AI compute, semiconductors, and software infrastructure; often drives index-level momentum and cross-sector sympathy.

subsectorProfiles.name: semiconductors
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: High beta, inventory-cycle-sensitive subsector that often becomes the first mover for broader growth risk appetite.

subsectorProfiles.name: software_infrastructure
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Theme-driven software cohort that tends to trend cleanly when broad growth multiples expand and enterprise demand narratives stay intact.

subsectorProfiles.name: ai_compute
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Narrative-dense subgroup where capex announcements and supply-chain constraints can create strong sympathy waves and crowded momentum behavior.

subsectorProfiles.name: cybersecurity
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Defensive-growth software pocket that reacts to breach headlines, federal-security spend, and platform-consolidation narratives with cleaner readthrough than broad software beta.

marketStructureConsiderations: watch opening auction breadth in top mega-caps
separate liquid leaders from thin thematic laggards
treat ETF-led reversals as sector-wide veto signals
complianceConsiderations: respect exchange halts on headline-sensitive AI names
trace policy version when export-control headlines modify sector eligibility

queryExamples: Which subsector leaders are driving technology breadth right now?
Show suppress and invalidate conditions for technology momentum setups.

sectors.summary: Blend of internet platforms, media, and telecom where ad demand, engagement trends, and policy headlines can rotate leadership quickly.

subsectorProfiles.name: internet_platforms
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Liquid large-cap group that can behave like growth tech while still carrying ad-cycle and regulatory-event sensitivity.

subsectorProfiles.name: advertising_media
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Narrative-sensitive cohort with stronger reaction to ad spend, consumer data, and campaign cycles than to pure infrastructure signals.

subsectorProfiles.name: telecom
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Lower-beta communications subgroup shaped by spectrum policy, network-upgrade capex, and defensive cash-flow rotation rather than pure growth narratives.

marketStructureConsiderations: distinguish telecom defensiveness from platform momentum
use large-cap breadth as primary regime signal
complianceConsiderations: trace event-source confirmation when regulatory hearings alter sector posture

queryExamples: Show platform names with sector-leader breakout potential.
What regulatory triggers currently suppress communication-services setups?

sectors.summary: Consumer-cyclicals sector where demand, rates, and event seasonality drive large performance splits between retailers, autos, travel, and housing-related groups.

subsectorProfiles.name: retail
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Catalyst-heavy subsector that reacts sharply to sales, margin, and inventory commentary, especially around seasonal windows.

subsectorProfiles.name: autos
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Rate-sensitive group with strong reaction to financing conditions, production updates, and EV adoption narrative shifts.

subsectorProfiles.name: travel_leisure
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Experience-heavy cyclical pocket where booking trends, fuel costs, and discretionary-demand swings drive fast leadership turnover.

subsectorProfiles.name: homebuilders
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Rates-sensitive housing cohort that reacts sharply to mortgage moves, order trends, and land or financing commentary.

marketStructureConsiderations: split basket between high-beta growth retail and defensive discounters
watch rate-sensitive opening reactions
complianceConsiderations: preserve event timestamps when sales updates alter sector posture

queryExamples: Which discretionary subsectors are aligned with current consumer data?
Show invalidate conditions for discretionary trend setups.

sectors.summary: Defensive sector used more for relative-strength rotation, slow trend confirmation, and risk-off basket interpretation than for headline momentum alone.

subsectorProfiles.name: food_beverage
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Steadier defensive group with slower but cleaner reaction to input-cost shifts and pricing power narratives.

subsectorProfiles.name: discount_retail
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Defensive consumer pocket that can trade with both staples and discretionary depending on stress regime and value-seeking behavior.

subsectorProfiles.name: household_personal_care
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Steady cash-flow subgroup driven by pricing power, shelf-share trends, and input-cost normalization rather than sharp event beta.

marketStructureConsiderations: lower-beta names need confirmation from breadth not single-name gaps
use sector ETF leadership as gating signal
guardrailFocus: avoid forced momentum assumptions on low-volatility names
complianceConsiderations: document mixed staples-discretionary classification assumptions for audit review

queryExamples: Show staples names leading current defensive rotation.
What conditions suppress staples breakout strategies?

sectors.summary: Rates- and credit-sensitive sector where banks, brokers, payments, and insurers can diverge sharply depending on curve shape and stress regime.

subsectorProfiles.name: banks
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Balance-sheet and deposit-sensitive cohort tightly linked to yield-curve, funding, and credit-stress narratives.

subsectorProfiles.name: brokers_exchanges
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Capital-markets subgroup that responds more directly to volume, volatility, and issuance conditions than to deposit risk.

subsectorProfiles.name: payments
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Transaction-volume and consumer-spend-sensitive subgroup that often bridges financials and discretionary behavior through volume and take-rate commentary.

subsectorProfiles.name: insurance
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Cash-flow and reserve-quality subgroup driven by rate backdrop, catastrophe risk, and underwriting commentary more than short-term beta.

marketStructureConsiderations: separate regional-bank stress from broad financial strength
treat macro release windows as hard routing overlays
complianceConsiderations: preserve policy provenance when rates events override default financial-sector eligibility

queryExamples: Which financial subsectors are aligned with current curve movement?
Show financial-sector invalidate conditions tied to credit stress.

sectors.summary: Mixed defensive and event-driven sector where biotech, pharma, devices, and managed care require different catalyst and halt-risk handling.

subsectorProfiles.name: biotech
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Binary-event-heavy pocket where trial, FDA, and funding events dominate price behavior and halt risk is elevated.

subsectorProfiles.name: medical_devices
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Higher-quality trending group that often trades with procedure volumes, hospital spending, and idiosyncratic earnings commentary.

subsectorProfiles.name: pharma
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Large-cap defensive-growth subgroup shaped by drug-launch cadence, pipeline updates, and patent or pricing-policy narratives.

subsectorProfiles.name: managed_care
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Policy-sensitive insurance and care-delivery pocket where reimbursement shifts and utilization trends drive rotational moves.

marketStructureConsiderations: split binary-event biotech from durable defensive healthcare
apply halt-risk scoring before momentum eligibility
complianceConsiderations: retain source and timestamp lineage for all health-care regulatory signals

queryExamples: Show health-care setups suppressed by current halt-risk scoring.
Which healthcare subsectors are trading as defensive quality rather than event beta?

sectors.summary: Cyclical sector with strong macro and geopolitical sensitivity through aerospace, defense, transport, machinery, and electrical infrastructure names.

subsectorProfiles.name: aerospace_defense
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Longer-duration policy and contract-driven group that reacts to defense budgets, geopolitical events, and supply-chain constraints.

subsectorProfiles.name: transportation_logistics
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Economic-barometer group tied to freight, travel, shipping, and margin conditions with strong sensitivity to fuel and demand trends.

subsectorProfiles.name: machinery
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Capex-driven industrial pocket that reflects order-book momentum, dealer inventory, and global-manufacturing sentiment.

subsectorProfiles.name: electrical_equipment
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Infrastructure-sensitive subgroup tied to grid modernization, factory automation, and power-demand investment cycles.

marketStructureConsiderations: separate defense policy momentum from pure cyclical industrial demand
use shipping and fuel overlays for transport names
complianceConsiderations: retain source lineage for sanctions or defense-policy headlines that alter eligibility

queryExamples: Which industrial subsectors benefit from current defense-policy headlines?
Show industrial setups invalidated by conflicting freight and fuel signals.

sectors.summary: Commodity-linked sector where crude, gas, shipping, and geopolitical disruptions can dominate price behavior and create fast regime changes.

subsectorProfiles.name: exploration_production
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: High beta energy pocket with direct sensitivity to commodity futures and geopolitical supply risk.

subsectorProfiles.name: oilfield_services
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Second-derivative subgroup tied to capex expectations and service pricing rather than spot commodity alone.

subsectorProfiles.name: integrated_oil
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Cash-rich mega-cap energy subgroup where capital-return policy, refining margins, and commodity stability matter as much as raw beta.

subsectorProfiles.name: renewables
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Policy- and rate-sensitive clean-energy subgroup that trades on subsidy frameworks, financing costs, and grid-demand themes.

marketStructureConsiderations: futures moves can pre-lead equity opens
headline freshness matters more than stale commodity direction
complianceConsiderations: preserve geopolitical source lineage and recheck official confirmations before activation

queryExamples: Which energy names are aligned with current commodity beta?
Show energy invalidate conditions tied to unresolved supply headlines.

sectors.summary: Materials sector with strong sensitivity to metals, chemicals, agricultural inputs, and global industrial demand signals.

subsectorProfiles.name: metals_mining
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Commodity-sensitive pocket driven by base metals, precious metals, and global industrial demand or supply shifts.

subsectorProfiles.name: chemicals
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Margin- and feedstock-sensitive subgroup that often moves on energy, industrial demand, and pricing-spread dynamics.

subsectorProfiles.name: packaging
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Steadier industrial-materials pocket influenced by consumer demand mix, freight costs, and contract repricing rather than raw commodity spikes alone.

marketStructureConsiderations: liquidity is less uniform than tech or financials
commodity futures can front-run equity reactions
complianceConsiderations: maintain source lineage for tariff and trade-policy overlays

queryExamples: Which materials subsectors are aligned with current metal-price momentum?
Show suppress conditions for materials setups under trade-policy conflict.

sectors.summary: Lower-beta sector used for defensive rotation, rate sensitivity, and grid or power-infrastructure themes rather than pure momentum leadership.

subsectorProfiles.name: regulated_utility
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Defensive income-heavy group most sensitive to rates, policy, and storm or outage headlines.

subsectorProfiles.name: grid_infrastructure
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Infrastructure buildout subgroup that can trade more like industrial growth when power demand narratives strengthen.

subsectorProfiles.name: power_generation
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Power-market subgroup that reacts to load forecasts, fuel spreads, and capacity updates more quickly than regulated utility rate cases.

marketStructureConsiderations: do not overfit momentum assumptions to low-volatility names
grid-infrastructure subgroup may trade differently from regulated utilities
complianceConsiderations: retain source lineage for regulatory approval or rate-case updates

queryExamples: Which utility names are aligned with current defensive rotation?
Show utility-related regulatory signals that suppress activation.

sectors.summary: Rate-sensitive sector where REITs and property exposure behave differently depending on financing costs, occupancy themes, and property-type leadership.

subsectorProfiles.name: reit_data_centers
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Growth-oriented REIT group with overlap to AI infrastructure and power-demand narratives rather than pure yield sensitivity.

subsectorProfiles.name: commercial_reit
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Higher-friction property group tied to financing conditions, occupancy trends, and macro stress rather than immediate growth themes.

subsectorProfiles.name: residential_reit
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Housing-linked REIT pocket driven by rent trends, turnover, and regional affordability rather than office-style credit stress alone.

marketStructureConsiderations: separate data-center REIT behavior from office or commercial stress narratives
use rate and credit overlays together
complianceConsiderations: keep financing and credit-source lineage for auditable sector overrides

queryExamples: Which REIT subsectors are aligned with current rate direction?
Show real-estate setups blocked by credit-stress conditions.

sectors.summary: Cross-asset sector linking miners, exchanges, and crypto-beta equities to digital-asset price regimes, liquidity conditions, and regulatory headlines.

subsectorProfiles.name: miners
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: High beta equity proxies to crypto spot and hash-price economics, often moving ahead of smaller beta names during strong crypto trends.

subsectorProfiles.name: exchanges
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Higher-quality crypto infrastructure names that react to volume, policy, and market-structure headlines more than to pure token beta alone.

subsectorProfiles.name: crypto_beta_equities
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Non-exchange equity proxies whose price action tracks broad digital-asset risk appetite, leverage conditions, and thematic spillover more than direct blockchain fundamentals.

subsectorProfiles.name: stablecoin_infrastructure
subsectorProfiles.behaviorProfile: Policy-sensitive fintech and settlement subgroup tied to reserve transparency, payments rails, and enforcement posture more than raw token beta.

marketStructureConsiderations: after-hours and overnight conditions matter more than for standard equities
digital-asset benchmark confirmation is required for most proxy trades
complianceConsiderations: preserve regulatory-source lineage and policy versions for all crypto-sector overrides

queryExamples: Which crypto proxies are aligned with current benchmark beta?
Show crypto-sector suppress or invalidate conditions tied to regulatory enforcement.
